steps.title: Use the horizontal intercept
steps.content: At the $x$-intercept of the graph, $y=0$, so there are no K-type stars. The total mass is then made only by the M-type stars.

steps.title: Divide by the star count
steps.content:
From the graph, the $x$-intercept is about $158$. If $m$ is the estimated mass of each M-type star, then the total mass divided by the number of M-type stars gives $m$:
$$158m=127{,}882$$
$$m=\frac{127{,}882}{158}=809.4$$

steps.title: Choose the closest mass
steps.content: The computed mass, $809.4$ quettagrams, is closest to $811$ quettagrams among the listed choices. The answer is A.
